prompt:
Localization ticket, the real one this time. We've outgrown flat catalog strings: translators need plural and gender branches INSIDE a message, and the Polish/Arabic plural math absolutely cannot live in product code. Give me an ICU-MessageFormat-style mini engine in one module, msgfmt.ts — parser plus formatter, TypeScript, zero dependencies. No HTML, no catalogs, no number formatting beyond String(n); this is message syntax and plural rules, nothing else.

Exports:
- class MessageFormat — `new MessageFormat(src, locale)` parses eagerly (ALL syntax errors surface at construction), `.format(values = {})` renders a string.
- pluralCategory(locale, n) — the plural-rules table, exported on its own because the l10n test-suite wants to hit it directly.
- class MessageFormatError extends Error, with numeric `.line` and `.col` (both 1-based) and message EXACTLY `parse error at <line>:<col>: <detail>`.

Locales: we ship en, pl, ar. Match on the language subtag — everything before the first '-', lowercased, so 'pl-PL' and 'EN' work. Anything else throws a plain Error `unsupported locale "<locale-as-passed>"` from both the constructor and pluralCategory.

Plural rules (cardinals; classify negatives by absolute value; ANY non-integer is 'other' in all three locales):
- en: 1 → one; everything else → other.
- pl: 1 → one; last digit 2–4 but last two digits NOT 12–14 → few; every other integer (0, 5–21, 12, 13, 14, 25, 112, …) → many.
- ar: 0 → zero; 1 → one; 2 → two; n%100 in 3–10 → few; n%100 in 11–99 → many; otherwise (100, 101, 102, 200, …) → other.

Syntax:
- Plain text passes through. `{name}` substitutes String(values.name). Argument names are [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*.
- `{count, plural, one {# file} other {# files} =0 {none}}` — selectors are the six category words or exact matches `=<digits>`. An exact match (n === that integer) beats the category; if the computed category has no branch, fall back to `other`, which is MANDATORY. Inside plural branches `#` renders the plural value as String(n) — and it stays live inside select branches nested in that plural (nearest enclosing plural wins; a nested plural rebinds it). Outside any plural, `#` is plain text.
- `{gender, select, male {…} female {…} other {…}}` — selectors are [A-Za-z0-9_]+ words; matches String(value) exactly, falls back to the mandatory `other`.
- Branches nest arbitrarily: every {…} body is full message syntax.
- Whitespace is free around names, commas and selectors: `{ n , plural , one {x} other {y} }` parses.
- Quoting, ICU rules: `''` is a literal apostrophe anywhere. An apostrophe immediately before `{`, `}`, or a live `#` opens a quoted literal that runs to the next lone apostrophe (`''` inside it is a literal apostrophe). Any other apostrophe is ordinary text — don't needs no escaping.
- Duplicate selectors in one argument are an error, not last-wins.

Parse errors — MessageFormatError with these exact detail strings, at these positions (line 1-based, col 1-based, and an error at end-of-input sits one column past the last character):
- `expected '}'` — argument or branch body never closed (at EOF), or junk after a simple argument's name (at the junk character).
- `empty argument name` — at the character where a name should have started.
- `unknown argument type "<type>"` — anything besides plural/select, at the type token's start.
- `expected ','` — plural/select with no branch list, at the offending character.
- `invalid selector "<tok>"` — at the selector's start (plural: not a category word or =digits; select: not [A-Za-z0-9_]+).
- `duplicate selector "<tok>"` — at the repeated selector's start.
- `expected '{' after selector` — at the character where the branch body should open.
- `plural requires an "other" branch` / `select requires an "other" branch` — at the argument's OPENING '{'.
- `unexpected '}'` — stray close brace in text, at the brace.
- `unclosed quoted literal` — at the opening apostrophe.

Format-time problems are plain Errors (not MessageFormatError): `unknown variable "count"` when values lacks an own property a simple/plural/select argument needs, and `plural argument "count" must be a number` when a plural argument's value is present but not a finite number.

Contract: node --test test_msgfmt.ts (node 26 type stripping: plain typed TS, no enums/namespaces). The test file is read-only.
